taxonomy: Clavula acicularis is a type of fungus, specifically belonging to the genus Clavulina and the family Clavulinaceae. The species is classified as a member of the phylum Basidiomycota. This classification places Clavula acicularis within the larger group of basidiomycete fungi, which are characterized by their unique reproductive structures. Further classification within the kingdom Fungi is also applicable, as Clavula acicularis is a member of this broader group. The exact classification of Clavula acicularis may vary slightly depending on the specific source or system used.
